prediction_status.summary: 18 predictions, 0 resolved. Most registered 2026-03-22 (1 day ago). Too early for full scoring but mid-course check reveals structural insights.

stagflation_thesis_check.diagnosis: Supply shock real but demand destruction absent. Slow-burn not acute crisis. Correlated portfolio means ~12/18 fail if demand destruction never arrives.

Q10_CPI.reason: Feb CPI 2.4% — well below 3.0% revision threshold. Early trigger.

Q10_CPI_naive.reason: Same CPI data invalidates naive base rate too.

ead_updates.Q07_10Y: WATCH — 4.41% approaching 4.5% trigger, no revision yet
ead_updates.Q05_WTI: NO REVISION — Hormuz still closed, thesis intact

key_finding: VIX declining despite elevated geopolitical risk suggests markets have priced in the supply shock. The swarm registered predictions AFTER the shock was priced in — a timing bias. The prediction portfolio's effective N~7 (not 18) is now empirically visible: predictions that should be independent share the same macro driver.
expect: Resolve PRED-0017. Bayesian update EAD checkpoints. Produce calibration lesson.
actual: PRED-0017 not yet resolvable (6 days remaining). Mid-course calibration reveals stagflation thesis half-right: supply shock real, demand destruction absent. 1 EAD early-trigger (Q10 CPI 0.40→0.10). VIX decline contradicts portfolio thesis.
diff: Expected PRED-0017 resolution — NOT YET (March 29 deadline). Unexpected: VIX declining despite geopolitical risk = markets priced in shock before predictions registered.
